questions.title: Add Minutes to a Time
questions.prompt:
Given a time in HH:MM 24-hour format and an integer number of minutes, return the new time in HH:MM format.

The answer should wrap correctly across midnight.

Example:
23:50 + 25 minutes = 00:15
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Add minutes to time"><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Add Minutes to a Time</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Convert time to total minutes, add, then convert back</text><rect x="62" y="118" width="170" height="84" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.26)"/><text x="147" y="170" text-anchor="middle" font-size="34" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">23:50</text><text x="268" y="170" font-size="28" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">+ 25 min</text><path d="M 420 158 L 530 158" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 516 144 L 530 158 L 516 172" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="566" y="118" width="170" height="84" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)"/><text x="651" y="170" text-anchor="middle" font-size="34" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">00:15</text><rect x="770" y="102" width="170" height="118" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="790" y="136" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Rule</text><text x="790" y="170" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">total = (hours*60 + minutes + add)</text><text x="790" y="198" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">mod 1440</text><text x="30" y="280" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">A full day has 1440 minutes.</text></svg>
hints: Split HH:MM into hours and minutes.
Convert time to total minutes.
Add the given minutes and take modulo 1440.
Convert back to HH:MM with zero-padding.
questions.starter_code:
def add_minutes(time_str, minutes_to_add):
    # TODO
    return ""

print(add_minutes("23:50", 25))
print(add_minutes("10:05", 125))
questions.solution_code:
def add_minutes(time_str, minutes_to_add):
    hh, mm = map(int, time_str.split(':'))
    total = hh * 60 + mm
    total = (total + minutes_to_add) % 1440
    new_h = total // 60
    new_m = total % 60
    return f"{new_h:02d}:{new_m:02d}"

print(add_minutes("23:50", 25))
print(add_minutes("10:05", 125))

questions.title: Minutes Between Two Times
questions.prompt:
Given two times in HH:MM format, return the number of minutes between them.

Assume the second time may be on the next day.

Example:
23:40 to 00:10 = 30 minutes
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Minutes between two times"><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Minutes Between Two Times</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">If end is smaller than start, treat it as next day</text><rect x="70" y="118" width="170" height="84" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.20)"/><text x="155" y="170" text-anchor="middle" font-size="34" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">23:40</text><path d="M 274 160 L 464 160" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 450 146 L 464 160 L 450 174" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="500" y="118" width="170" height="84" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)"/><text x="585" y="170" text-anchor="middle" font-size="34" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">00:10</text><rect x="734" y="102" width="184" height="118" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="754" y="136" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Answer</text><text x="754" y="180" font-size="30" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">30 min</text><text x="30" y="280" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Formula: if end < start, add 1440 to end before subtracting.</text></svg>
hints: Convert both times to minutes.
If end minutes < start minutes, add 1440 to end.
Subtract start from end.
Return the difference.
questions.starter_code:
def minutes_between(start, end):
    # TODO
    return 0

print(minutes_between("23:40", "00:10"))
print(minutes_between("09:15", "11:00"))
questions.solution_code:
def minutes_between(start, end):
    sh, sm = map(int, start.split(':'))
    eh, em = map(int, end.split(':'))

    start_total = sh * 60 + sm
    end_total = eh * 60 + em

    if end_total < start_total:
        end_total += 1440

    return end_total - start_total

print(minutes_between("23:40", "00:10"))
print(minutes_between("09:15", "11:00"))

questions.title: Day of Week After N Days
questions.prompt:
Given a day name and an integer n, return the day after n days.

Use these day names:
Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Day of week after n days"><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Day of Week After N Days</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Use index + modulo 7</text><g font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="48" y="126" width="110" height="58" rx="12" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.14)"/><text x="103" y="163" text-anchor="middle">Mon</text><rect x="170" y="116" width="110" height="78" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)"/><text x="225" y="163" text-anchor="middle">Tue</text><rect x="292" y="126" width="110" height="58" rx="12" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.14)"/><text x="347" y="163" text-anchor="middle">Wed</text><rect x="414" y="126" width="110" height="58" rx="12" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.14)"/><text x="469" y="163" text-anchor="middle">Thu</text><rect x="536" y="116" width="110" height="78" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.24)"/><text x="591" y="163" text-anchor="middle">Fri</text></g><text x="170" y="230" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Tuesday + 10 days = Friday</text><rect x="720" y="108" width="190" height="120" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="740" y="142" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Rule</text><text x="740" y="178" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">(start_index + n) % 7</text><text x="30" y="282" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">This is a cyclic array problem.</text></svg>
hints: Store the weekdays in a list.
Find the index of the starting day.
Compute (index + n) % 7.
Return the weekday at that index.
questions.starter_code:
def day_after(day, n):
    # TODO
    return ""

print(day_after("Tuesday", 10))
print(day_after("Sunday", 1))
questions.solution_code:
def day_after(day, n):
    days = ["Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"]
    idx = days.index(day)
    return days[(idx + n) % 7]

print(day_after("Tuesday", 10))
print(day_after("Sunday", 1))

questions.title: Number of Days Between Two Dates
questions.prompt:
Given two dates in DD-MM-YYYY format, return the absolute difference in days.

You must handle leap years correctly.

Leap year rule:
- divisible by 400 → leap year
- divisible by 100 only → not leap year
- divisible by 4 only → leap year
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1040 360" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Number of days between two dates"><rect width="1040" height="360" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Number of Days Between Two Dates</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Convert each date to total days from a fixed origin, then subtract</text><rect x="60" y="120" width="230" height="92" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.22)"/><text x="175" y="157" text-anchor="middle" font-size="30" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">27-03-2026</text><path d="M 320 166 L 500 166" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 486 152 L 500 166 L 486 180" fill="none" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/><rect x="530" y="120" width="230" height="92" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.34)"/><text x="645" y="157" text-anchor="middle" font-size="30" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">01-04-2026</text><rect x="802" y="104" width="198" height="138" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="822" y="138" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Need to handle</text><text x="822" y="174" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">month lengths</text><text x="822" y="202" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">leap years</text><text x="822" y="230" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">absolute difference</text><text x="60" y="300" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Answer = 5 days</text></svg>

Write a function to count total days before a date.
Use month lengths and adjust February in leap years.
Return the absolute difference of the two totals.

questions.title: Meeting Overlap Duration
questions.prompt:
Given two meeting intervals in HH:MM-HH:MM format, return how many minutes they overlap.

If they do not overlap, return 0.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1040 360" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Meeting overlap duration"><rect width="1040" height="360" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Meeting Overlap Duration</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Overlap = min(end times) - max(start times)</text><line x1="90" y1="180" x2="940" y2="180" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/><rect x="210" y="140" width="280" height="34" rx="12" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.26)"/><text x="350" y="134" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">10:15 - 11:00</text><rect x="350" y="188" width="280" height="34" rx="12" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.20)"/><text x="490" y="248" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">10:40 - 11:20</text><rect x="350" y="150" width="140" height="62" rx="16" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.40)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.16)"/><text x="420" y="294" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Overlap = 20 min</text><text x="92" y="210" font-size="14" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">left</text><text x="914" y="210" font-size="14" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">right</text><rect x="760" y="104" width="220" height="118" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="780" y="138" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Formula</text><text x="780" y="174" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">start = max(s1, s2)</text><text x="780" y="202" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">end = min(e1, e2)</text></svg>
hints: Convert both intervals into start and end minutes.
Overlap starts at max(start1, start2).
Overlap ends at min(end1, end2).
If end <= start, overlap is 0.
questions.starter_code:
def overlap_minutes(interval1, interval2):
    # TODO
    return 0

print(overlap_minutes("10:15-11:00", "10:40-11:20"))
print(overlap_minutes("09:00-09:30", "10:00-10:15"))
questions.solution_code:
def to_minutes(time_str):
    hh, mm = map(int, time_str.split(':'))
    return hh * 60 + mm


def overlap_minutes(interval1, interval2):
    a_start, a_end = interval1.split('-')
    b_start, b_end = interval2.split('-')

    s1 = to_minutes(a_start)
    e1 = to_minutes(a_end)
    s2 = to_minutes(b_start)
    e2 = to_minutes(b_end)

    start = max(s1, s2)
    end = min(e1, e2)

    return max(0, end - start)

print(overlap_minutes("10:15-11:00", "10:40-11:20"))
print(overlap_minutes("09:00-09:30", "10:00-10:15"))
